WARNING: These test points are for A2681 (820-02536) ONLY. For other boards, use that board's diagnostic files. Component designators (C5704, C8450, C8440, etc.) are board-specific — the same rail appears at DIFFERENT components on other boards.
description: Decision trees for all 5V DCPS scenarios. Board connected via USB-C at 5V (pre-PD negotiation). Current draw indicates where in the power chain the fault lies.
data_source: 198 ammeter readings at 5V from safan_diagnostics_parsed.json + Ricky's step-by-step workflow

scenarios.meaning: Board draws nothing. CD3217 is not even loading the VBUS line. Fault is at the input stage — connector, cable, or CD3217 itself.

steps.expected: Clean pins, no bent/missing contacts, no liquid residue
steps.if_wrong: Replace USB-C connector flex or clean connector
DCPS cable or connector open circuit. Try other port (CF501 for ATC1). If both dead, connector flex fault.

steps.measure: Diode mode on UF400 VDD pins
steps.if_wrong: UF400 short to ground → replace CD3217. Known fault: ROM short can kill CD3217 (see Saf data: UF400 + ROM short).
CF408 shorted → remove and retest. If short clears, replace cap. If short persists, CD3217 (UF400) is the source.
steps.data_note: CF408 appears in 4 fault records in Saf's data

steps.measure: Move DCPS to second port, probe CF501
steps.if_wrong: Both ports dead → likely upstream issue (connector flex, liquid damage to both CD3217s)
steps.if_ok: First port CD3217 (UF400) fault. Second port works → replace UF400.

scenarios.meaning: CD3217 is drawing some current but board is not progressing through boot. Most common 5V scenario. The 5V is reaching the charger circuit but PPBUS_AON is likely not coming up, or it's coming up but PP3V8_AON is not.

steps.expected: 12V (battery voltage) or rising toward ~5V (adapter only, pre-PD)
steps.if_missing: Fault in charging circuit. Go to step 2.
steps.data_note: From Ricky's workflow: 'NO → fault in charging circuit / CD3217 / U5200'

steps.measure: Diode mode on PPBUS_AON (C5704 or CR765)
steps.if_wrong: PPBUS_AON shorted. Isolate: remove CR765, retest. If short clears, CR765 was source. If short persists, trace downstream. Known fault: CR734 short on PPBUS_AON (see Saf data: 5V/0.014A → CR734 replace → fixed).
steps.next_if_ok: Check charger IC (U5200 area) and CD3217 communication. CD3217 may not be sending CHGR_AUX_OK.

steps.if_missing: Fault in U5700 (SVR AON) or Q5800. Check PPVAON_PWR_EN (enable) — if not asserted, PMU not requesting 3V8. Check for short on PP3V8_AON line.
steps.data_note: From Ricky's workflow: 'NO → fault in U5700 / Q5800'

steps.action: Check PP3V8_AON for short on VDDMAIN
steps.if_wrong: PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN shorted. Known fault location. Thermal cam to find hot component. C7701 also on PP3V8_AON — check C7701, C7702, C7714 (PP3V8_AON_SPMU_ISNS).
steps.data_note: C7705 appears in 3 fault records. C7714 (PP3V8_AON_SPMU_ISNS) shorted at 5V/0.12A in Saf data.

steps.if_missing: Check C8430 (diode — shorted?), L8430 (open?). Replace faulty component, retest.

steps.if_all_present: Fault is downstream of PMU — SoC domain or peripheral. Thermal cam.

steps.measure: Apply thermal cam to the rail that is missing/shorted
steps.if_found: Replace hot chip. Known: PP1V8_AON shorted → suspect UC870.

scenarios.meaning: Board is drawing moderate current at 5V. Charger circuit is partially working. PP3V8_AON is likely present but something downstream is loading it or a short is holding the board in a partial state. PD negotiation may not be completing.

steps.if_missing: Charger path fault. See 0.001-0.05A tree step 2.

steps.if_wrong: PP3V8_AON_SPMU_ISNS shorted. Remove C7714 and retest. Known fault at 5V/0.12A in Saf data.

steps.if_found: Identify and replace hot component. Common: C7701, C7702 area.
steps.data_note: Saf: 5V/0.14A → C7702 short; 5V/0.23A → PP3V8_AON + PP3V8_AON_SPMU_ISNS dual short (C7701)

steps.measure: Probe C8440 (5V) and PP3V3_S2 nearby cap
steps.if_3v3_missing: Check PP3V3_S2_LDO line. Known fault: CF660 short on PP3V3_S2_LDO at 5V/0.22A.

scenarios.range: 0.2–0.5A (significant current, boot stalled or shorted rail)
scenarios.meaning: Board is drawing significant current at 5V. Either: (A) board is trying to boot but stalling (possibly waiting for PD to negotiate 20V), or (B) a rail downstream of PP3V8_AON has a short pulling current. Common: PP3V3_S2 short, PP3V8_AON branch short.

steps.action: Is the board trying to boot? Check for signs of life.
steps.measure: Look for fan spin, keyboard backlight, display backlight flash, chime
steps.if_signs: Board is booting at 5V but not completing PD negotiation. Check CD3217 firmware ROM (SPI_UPC0). If ROM corrupted, CD3217 can't negotiate 20V. Try known-good ROM. Go to step 5.
steps.if_no_signs: Board is drawing current into a short or stalled PMU. Go to step 2.

steps.if_any_missing: Fault identified at the missing rail. See 0.001-0.05A tree for that specific rail.

steps.action: Check PP3V3_S2 and PP3V3_UPC LDO lines for shorts
steps.measure: Diode mode on PP3V3_S2 line, PP3V3_UPC0_LDO, PP3V3_UPC1_LDO
steps.if_wrong: PP3V3 rail shorted. Known fault: Saf 5V/0.223A → PP3V3_UPC1_LDO + PP3V3_S2 short → CD3217 causing the short.
steps.data_note: Saf: 5V/0.223A → PP3V3_UPC1_LDO + PP3V3_S2 short (CD chip fault)

steps.measure: Diode mode on PP3V8_AON distribution caps
steps.if_wrong: PP3V8_AON short at 0.2-0.5A. Known: Saf 5V/0.443A → CT610 on PP3V8_AON line shorted.

steps.action: Connect to 20V DCPS and check if board transitions
steps.if_normal_boot: Board boots at 20V. Problem was CD3217 PD negotiation. Replace SPI ROM or CD3217.
steps.if_same_current: Short is genuine. Thermal cam all power domains.

scenarios.range: 0.5A+ (high current at 5V — major short or boot attempt)
scenarios.meaning: Very high current at 5V. Either a serious short on a high-current rail (PP3V8_AON, PPBUS_AON) or the board is attempting to boot and pulling significant power. At 5V, >0.5A is unusual and suggests a short.

steps.expected: Current should drop when battery disconnected
steps.note: High current at 5V with battery can cause heating. Disconnect battery and test on DCPS only.

steps.if_wrong: PPBUS_AON dead short. Thermal cam immediately. Suspect charger IC area, battery connector, or bulk cap failure.

steps.measure: Diode mode on C8450 and C7705
steps.if_wrong: PP3V8_AON dead short. U5700 pulling max current trying to regulate into a short. Thermal cam → find hot component in PP3V8_AON distribution (C7705 VDDMAIN area, WiFi module PP3V8_AON_WLBT).

steps.action: Check if board is actually booting
steps.expected: If board boots normally at 20V with 1.5-2.5A, the 5V high current was a boot attempt. PD negotiation working.
steps.if_boots: Normal. Board was trying to boot at 5V. No fault.
steps.if_same_high_current: Genuine short. Systematic thermal cam sweep of all power stages.
